title: Divine & Demoniac Qualities | BG 16.01 - 16.03 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: The name of title is divine and demoniac qualities. So today's discussion will be
based on the wonderful purpose teachings of His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta
Swami Prabhupada. This is text 4 from the 16th chapter. Arrogance,
pride, anger, conceit, harshness and ignorance. These qualities belong to those of demoniac
nature, O son of Pritha. So we talked about the chapter name, it is divine and demoniac
qualities. So here Krishna is talking about the qualities which are manifest in the behaviour
of a demoniac person.
So,
it is mentioned that they are arrogant or proud in possessing. Suppose somebody has
some talent, some skill, some abilities, some wealth, something in possession. There is
an element of pride. I have done it. I have made it in life. I have accumulated this asset.
And this also brings an element of arrogance.
Unlike these qualities which are manifest in a demoniac person, a divine person in his
consciousness believes. Although he may have property papers, he may go and sign the thing.
In an official level, yes, this is my property. But deep within he believes that it is all
Everything belongs to the Lord. Unlike that, a demoniac person thinks that he has made it. He is the cause of his accomplishment.
Now, there is a difference between demanding respect and commanding respect. So, the demoniac demands respect, whereas a divine person, based on his divine qualities, commands respect.
He is not asking for respect, but seeing his wonderful qualities, invariably, he demands respect.
He draws respect. A demoniac person, invariably, for small, small things, gets angry.
And each one of us have a mix of divine and demoniac qualities.
As we become devotees, the percentage of divine qualities will increase.
And this is one of the measurements that, yes, we have.
We are advancing in our spiritual journey.
If we look back, now somebody says, how do we know that we are advancing?
The qualities what we are discussing today, the demoniac qualities which we may have, will eventually, in due course of time, if we are on the right path and advancing, will diminish.
And the divine quality will start manifesting in our behaviour.
So, one of the qualities of demoniac are, they get angry, small, small things.
They get irritated, agitated.
Whereas a divine person is very calm, very sober.
He is very tolerant.
This reminds me of a story of a person who was a very exalted devotee.
And as a result, his fame.
He became quite famous and people from different villages would come to meet him, have his darshan, pay obeisances to him.
So, demoniac people, they don't like this.
So, one such demoniac person somehow wanted to bring dis-respute to this saintly person.
He wanted to show to the people that he is just a farce.
He is just putting up a show.
Actually, he is not a divine person.
So, this person hired a person and gave him an assignment that I will pay you so and so amount if you could somehow make him get angry.
Make him lose temper.
And then when he loses temper in public, in front of everyone, he will be exposed.
People will see that it's all a show.
He is also short tempered.
He also gets agitated, irritated.
Small, small things.
So, this person who was hired went to the river, quite close to the place where this person used to do his meditation.
And on his way back, passed by that way and spat at him.
Spit at him.
So, this person, if we were there, what will we do?
We will get angry. We will get agitated.
How dare you?
So, this person looks at him, smiles and then gets up, goes to the river and takes his bath.
And again comes back and sits in his place.
Again, the second time, the same person comes and again does the same thing.
Again, this person goes to the river.
In fact, sorry, this person had gone to the river.
He was returning back home and this person spit at him.
So, this repeated, you won't believe, 108 times.
Every time he would spit at him, he would smile, go back to the river, take his bath and then come back.
When this happened, the 108th time, this person who was hired to make him angry fell at his feet.
He says, I am very, very sorry.
I stooped to such low heights.
Low heights just for the greed of money.
And then he started disclosing that how he was paid so and so amount.
And then he says, I am so sorry that I had to make you take bath 108 times.
But I am really surprised.
Every time, he didn't get angry.
He just went.
Then this person said, why you have to be sorry?
It is a great blessing today that I have got an opportunity to take bath in the river 108 times.
And then he says, in fact, I am sorry.
Had you told me that you are going to get such big amount, I would have acted as if I am getting angry.
I wouldn't have let you wait for so long.
Wasted so much of your time.
Again, we don't have to do this extreme.
By the way, does anger have a place in life?
Should we not get angry at all?
What is the answer?
Anger has a place? Yes? No?
How many of you say anger is important?
Sometimes.
How many of you say we should not get angry?
Anger is an enemy.
Anger kills, it burns within.
We should never get angry.
So, it is a big topic.
But there is a place for anger in our life.
And there is something called as rightful anger.
When a teacher gets angry at a child for right reason,
when he is not doing his homework or probably not mending his ways, not improving in his studies or something,
there is a place for it.
Parents getting angry has a place.
In fact, devotees also, we have many examples of exalted devotees getting angry.
Example?
Anumar, yes.
He got angry, he burned the whole Lanka.
Anybody else?
Durvasa is not a right example.
Who else?
Lord Chaitanya himself.
Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, when Nithyananda Prabhu, his associate was
was
somebody threw stone at him, hurt him, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu became very angry.
It is said that when somebody blasphemes the devotee or the Lord, one should become angry.
So, I think we will not get into details. It is a big subject.
Now, demoniac people do things whimsically.
They are driven by their opinions.
According to me, this is right.
In my opinion, I think so.
And suppose you tell a demoniac person, don't do this, it is not right thing.
You don't like, you don't do it.
If you tell a demoniac person, don't eat non-veg, I like it, I do it.
You don't like, you don't do it.
Who cares?
This is the spirit.
Now, what is good, what is bad, what is right, what is wrong, what is papa, what is punya
is not subject to our whims.
It is not what I think is right.
There are well-defined laws operating in this nature.
For example, traffic laws.
In India, we drive on the left side of the road.
In the US, it is on the right side.
It is not subject to my opinion, my likes.
I cannot be driving on the left side and then say, in my opinion, I think so.
If I do that, I will be penalized.
So, demoniac people do not care that there are higher laws.
I should act responsibly.
I like doing it, I enjoy it, I will do it.
I don't care.
I act whimsically.
Do not recognize any higher laws, higher authority.
Their actions are being witnessed.
Lord is witness to their action.
Accountability to their actions.
Existence of law of karma.
If I do wrong, I will be punished.
I will have to suffer the consequences.
They don't believe.
Eat, drink, be merry, enjoy life.
Whatever you like, do it.
They are very focused in the present.
Somebody tells, in future you will be punished.
There will be consequences.
Who cares?
I am enjoying.
Text 5.
The transcendental qualities are conducive to liberation whereas the demoniac qualities make for bondage.
Do not worry, O son of Pandu, for you are born with divine qualities.
Now, here Krishna, although he is imploring Arjuna to fight, he is saying that do not worry.
It is not because you have demoniac qualities, therefore you are getting angry and taking up this fight, this war.
You are born with divine qualities.
But for xyz reason, which he is going to share, you should fight.
Not because you have demonic qualities.
Not because you are short tempered.
Not because you are intolerant.
Not because you are very much looking forward for the kingdom.
Greed.
Driven by greed and lust.
No.
You are born with divine qualities.
Now, a question to all of you.
We will pause for a while.
Have you noticed, sometimes in the same house, brothers or sisters, siblings.
One of them is relatively very sober, very calm, very tolerant.
Apparently divine qualities.
And in the same house, another child is having, relatively speaking, demoniac qualities.
Anyone noticed?
Anywhere, in any circumstances.
May not be in your house, in your neighbour's house.
Why it happens?
Or sometimes, parents have divine qualities.
The son has, relatively speaking, demoniac qualities.
Or vice versa.
Give me an example of vice versa.
Prahlad Maharaj.
His father was demoniac.
Hirnakashyapu.
And son was Prahlad.
In fact, when mother of Prahlad.
What was her name?
Kadyu.
Kadyu or...
She was about to...
She was carrying Prahlad.
The Devatas wanted to kill her.
Because, thinking that she is carrying the son of Hirnakashyapu.
Who will be also a big demon.
So, why is it that some children are born with demoniac qualities.
Some children are born with divine qualities.
I know one family.
They have two sons.
One of them, elder son is...
You know, very, very quiet, calm, composed.
By chance, he is playing with a friend.
Somebody hits him.
He will not give back.
He will come back and report to his parents.
Mujhe Maharaj.
And, you know, relatively speaking, quite tolerant.
And this younger brother...
Is just the opposite.
He is three or four years younger to his...
This elder brother.
But, if elder brother's friends hit him.
This young guy will go and hit them.
And for small, small things, he will pick up quarrel.
And the parents were telling me that...
Now, I really wonder...
How much difference is there between the two?
What could be the reason?
But, same family.
Each one's karma is different.
Yes, this is a very important point.
That each one of us, although born in a particular family.
We are individual souls having our own baggage of karmic reactions.
Bhagavatam gives example that how the straws which are floating in water.
With the wave, they come together.
And with another wave, they disperse.
So, that way, yes.
We are not eternally related in a particular family.
We have come together in this life.
As brothers, sisters, father, mother.
And in due course of time.
We will segregate, we will separate.
Okay, this is one reason.
We are all individual souls.
What could be the other reason?
Very good.
The company you keep.
If you are in...
So, again, another example.
A person in Mumbai, in Powai.
He works with us.
I was travelling with him.
So, he says that he was posted in Punjab.
When he was working.

That movement was very...

All that unrest.
And after few years.
The younger son was born.

The younger...

His nature is completely different.

Very silent.

He is very quiet.

Mould.

So, it really affects a person's mentality.
His character and things like that.
So, that is second reason.
Third reason.
Prarabdh karma.
